summary.snippet: "Excellent service and staff and excellent experience from start to finish."
"Its a one stop place for dental work which is great for busy people."

most_relevant.description:
I had such a great experience at this dental office. I was nervous to come to a new dentist because in the past I've had dentists who lacked empathy, took shortcuts, or didn't take the time to explain anything.

I'm so glad I got to work with Dr. Taudel. She's excellent at what she does -- super thorough and detail oriented. And in addition, she had great bedside manner, checked in to make sure I wasn't in pain, etc. My procedure required multiple visits and I felt great about returning to see her and the team. All of the dental hygienists were great and the front desk staff were also very friendly. Super grateful for this experience!!

I can't express how grateful I am that I went to Tend Upper West Side. I have had terrible experiences at dentists' offices in the past, so I was very anxious leading up to this appointment.

Dr. Park and Channel were absolutely wonderful. They made me feel so comfortable, and reassured me that everything was going to go well - which it did! I didn't feel any pain at all. Dr. Park and Channel worked together flawlessly: fast, thorough, and efficient. They understood how nervous I was, and really took the time to address that. During my filling, Channel grabbed my hand for support, which meant so much to me. Dr. Park checked in frequently, making sure I was comfortable. When it was over, I shed tears of relief. The whole experience was so amazing, I cannot recommend Tend enough.

I had a follow up appointment with the dental hygienist, Khadija, who was amazing as well. While I was with Khadija, Dr. Park and Channel stopped in to say hello which made the experience so personable.

From the moment I walked into Tend to the moment I walked out, my feelings about the dentist flipped. I went from dread to feeling comfortable, and that's all thanks to the staff at Tend Upper West Side. This includes the front office staff as well, who are always so warm and welcoming. I highly recommend this Tend location!

I’m not sure where to even begin with this review. I had a dental emergency on what happened to be my birthday. I chipped a tooth and was in full panic-mode. I was able to make an emergency dental appointment at the UWS Tend location almost within an hour of the tooth chip. Dentist visits make me anxious in general, so I walked in very nervous. To make a very long story short, the dental hygienists at this location held my hand, wiped my tears, and consoled me when I had a full on panic attack during treatment. I lost my brother pretty traumatically recently and the lights, sounds, and laughing gas/oxygen mask all brought me back to being in the hospital with him. It wasn’t pretty and I will forever be grateful for the team at Tend for taking such great care of me. After a few very long hours, I was met in the lobby of the office by all the dental hygienists and the sweet receptionist who gave me a dozen roses, because it was my birthday. We all started crying together as I burst into tears. I’m still trying to process all of this. This was the first time I have been to this dentist office! Needless to say, my faith in humanity was restored. This review sounds fake and I assure you it is not. I’ve been in NYC for 25 years and things like this have become very few and far between. So thank you seems totally insufficient to the Tend team for all you did for me on a tough day and in a tough situation ❤️‍🩹.

The atmosphere here is very relaxing. The headphones they provide you with to listen to a show is calming and really makes the process go smoothly. I can appreciate the staffs welcoming attitude as well as how quick and efficient the dentists and hygienists are. Dr. Park is incredibly nice, thorough, and always leaves room for questions to be asked! Would absolutely recommend Tend to anyone who gets anxious at the dentist office!

Please, please trust your instincts and avoid chain dentistry. TL;DR, Tend UWS coerced me into five cavity fillings when no tooth decay was present. After the fourth, I got wise and sought two other outside opinions, only to learn that there was absolutely 0 tooth decay happening. Useless fillings and money spent.

After missing two 6mo dental appointments due to my moving cities, I finally settled down and scheduled an appointment at Tend UWS. Within the first ten minutes of my visit, the hygienist took X-Rays, glanced at my teeth, and mentioned that I would need FOUR fillings. As someone who has never had a cavity in my life, I was shocked to hear that just two missed cleanings opened me up to four cavities.

Assuming that the worst could absolutely be true given my lapse in care, I scheduled the fillings right away. They also mentioned two other potentially vulnerable teeth and gave me a prescription toothpaste to prevent any change there.

I completed four fillings and scheduled a check-in. At my check-in, a (different) hygienist told me that a FIFTH tooth had succumbed to a cavity and I would need a fifth filling. Shocked (since I was doing everything right), I decided not to schedule the fifth filling and pursue two outside opinions. I spent the next two 6 month check-ups with different dentists so that I could get two second/third opinions covered by insurance.

In looking at the fifth tooth, both dentists were shocked. They said that no tooth in my mouth showed any signs of decay; sure, a bit of discoloration as I have deep grooves, but absolutely no stickiness or decay. They were pretty certain, upon me telling my tale, that I was coerced into fillings I didn't need. The fifth tooth CERTAINLY did not need a filling -- the correct care plan would be to do nothing; the extra-careful care plan would be one sealant (which I ended up doing, mostly out of an abundance of caution).

This afternoon, I had my second non-Tend dentist appointment where I received great, honest care. The doctor today said to me outright that I got unnecessary work done. Her assistant called Tend "McDental" and I couldn't help but think about how much more I'd trust McDonald's to give me an honest chicken nugget than I would Tend to give me an honest cleaning.

Run!

Tend UWS responded to my dental emergency with care and compassion. While I experienced initial frustration with the administrative side of things, the extraordinarily high quality of the dental treatment I received is what's driving my 5-star review.

If I could, I would give Dr. Kate Pickett 10 stars. She deployed her brilliant strategic and creative skills to solve a very complex problem, and she did so with care and efficiency. Dr. Pickett made things happen that I thought would be impossible, always with a gracious smile. Ask for her, personally, if you have a choice of dentists!

Avoid for anything beyond basic cosmetic touch-ups

I went to this dental office for a check-up and regular cleaning. The place looks clean with an Instagram-like setup (super modern, aesthetic waiting area, friendly staff) and the hygienist was genuinely nice and thorough with the cleaning. That part was actually pretty good.

But the actual dentist experience was one of the worst I’ve had in my life.

The dentist spent only a few minutes in the room. She had a dismissive, rushed, and arrogant attitude. She didn’t explain anything about what she saw on the X-rays, didn’t describe my situation or the reasons for her recommended treatments. She just mentioned what she was adding to the “to do” list, completely ignoring the preferences I clearly stated and not even discussing if there were any alternatives.

It felt like a sales pitch from the moment she walked in, not like someone interested in my actual health or preferences. As soon as I mentioned TMJ discomfort, the first thing out of her mouth was suggesting Botox injections. She also recommended a crown for a “not ideal” fix that’s been perfectly fine for 15 years (no issues, no pain, no cracks), “because it won’t last much if you have bruxism.”

The ugly cherry on top: when I asked for my X-rays so I wouldn’t have to repeat the whole ordeal at a new dentist, they sent me a low-resolution PDF that’s practically useless for any other professional to read.

Bottom line: If you want a cleaning or cosmetic consult, maybe this place is fine.
But if you have any actual dental health concerns, want real explanations or thoughtful care, go somewhere else! This was the most unprofessional and frustrating dental visit I’ve ever had.

My boyfriend is very hesitant of the dentist and he had a decent amount of work to be done. They were wonderful at making him feel as comfortable as possible. They worked with him at a pace he was comfortable with. Mary O. his dental hygienist was absolutely amazing and she talked to both of us, laughed with both of us, and was so kind during the appointment even though I was just there for emotional support. I am now planning on also coming to this dentist.
